title: oxytropis campestris var. rydbergii
intro: Oxytropis campestris var. rydbergii, also known as Rydberg's mountain milkvetch, is a plant species in the legume family, Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system. Oxytropis campestris var. rydbergii can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in tincture form, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
